notes: Reproduced: SKSvgCanvas output is missing the closing </svg> tag when the underlying stream is read before the canvas is disposed. This is by-design behavior — SKSvgCanvas writes the closing </svg> tag during Dispose(). The reporter's code uses 'using var' for all three objects (MemoryStream, SKManagedWStream, SKSvgCanvas) and reads managedStream.ToArray() before the canvas is disposed (since 'using var' defers disposal to end of enclosing scope). The workaround is to explicitly dispose the canvas before reading the stream. Tested 2.88.9, 3.119.0, 3.119.2, and main — behavior is identical on all versions, contradicting the reporter's claim that 2.88.9 worked. The reporter's follow-up noting SKFileWStream 'works' is likely because they read the file after the scope ended (i.e., after disposal). This is not an SKManagedWStream-specific bug — it's a disposal ordering issue.

versionResults.notes: Reporter's 'last known good' version. Same behavior: missing </svg> when read before disposal.
Main branch source. Same behavior — not a regression.

reproductionSteps.description: Create a standalone console project with reporter's SkiaSharp version (3.119.0)
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 3.119.0
reproductionSteps.output:
The template "Console App" was created successfully.
Restored Repro.csproj.

filesCreated.description: Console project targeting net8.0 with SkiaSharp 3.119.0

reproductionSteps.description: Write reproduction code that mimics the reporter's pattern: uses 'using var' for MemoryStream, SKManagedWStream, and SKSvgCanvas, then reads the stream BEFORE canvas disposal. Also includes a correct pattern (explicit disposal before reading) for comparison.

filesCreated.description: Reproduction with two tests: reporter's pattern (read before disposal) and correct pattern (dispose then read)

reproductionSteps.description: Run the reproduction. Test 1 (reporter's pattern) shows missing </svg> tag (264 bytes, ends with False). Test 2 (correct pattern) shows complete SVG with </svg> tag (271 bytes, ends with True). This confirms the reported behavior.
reproductionSteps.output:
SkiaSharp Version: 3.119.0.0

=== Test 1: Reporter's pattern (read before canvas disposal) ===
SVG length: 264 bytes
Ends with </svg>: False
Last 100 chars: ...d" x="10" y="10" width="90" height="90"/>
	<ellipse fill="blue" cx="150" cy="150" rx="40" ry="40"/>


=== Test 2: Correct pattern (dispose canvas first, then read) ===
SVG length: 271 bytes
Ends with </svg>: True
Last 100 chars: ...0" y="10" width="90" height="90"/>
	<ellipse fill="blue" cx="150" cy="150" rx="40" ry="40"/>
</svg>

SUCCESS: test completed

reproductionSteps.description: Test with 2.88.9 (reporter's 'last known good' version) in a fresh project. Same behavior — missing </svg> when read before disposal, complete when disposed first. The claim that 2.88.9 worked is not confirmed.
reproductionSteps.output:
SkiaSharp Version: 2.88.0.0
=== Test 1: Reporter's pattern (read before canvas disposal) ===
SVG length: 264 bytes
Ends with </svg>: False
=== Test 2: Correct pattern (dispose canvas first, then read) ===
SVG length: 271 bytes
Ends with </svg>: True
SUCCESS: test completed

reproductionSteps.output:

=== Test 1: Reporter's pattern ===

=== Test 2: Correct pattern ===

errorMessages.primaryError: SVG output from SKSvgCanvas is missing closing </svg> tag when the underlying stream is read before the canvas is disposed

actionability.reason: Reproduced on all tested versions (2.88.9, 3.119.0, 3.119.2, main) — this is not a regression. The behavior is by-design: SKSvgCanvas writes the closing </svg> tag during Dispose(). The reporter's code reads the stream before the canvas is disposed due to 'using var' scope. The workaround is to explicitly dispose the canvas before reading.

workarounds: Dispose the SKSvgCanvas explicitly before reading the stream: call canvas.Dispose() (or use a using block) before managedStream.ToArray()
Replace 'using var canvas = ...' with a 'using (var canvas = ...) { }' block so disposal happens at the end of the block, then read the stream outside the block

proposedResponse.summary: Reproduced as user-error: stream read before canvas disposal; workaround provided.

actions.comment: Reproduced: missing </svg> when stream is read before canvas disposal. This is by-design — SVG close tag is emitted on Dispose(). Workaround: use a using block or explicitly dispose the canvas before reading the stream.

actions.description: Close as working-as-designed with workaround provided
Add docs-gap label since this is a common usage pitfall that could benefit from documentation
